summary: The diff implements most accessibility requirements including ARIA labels, keyboard navigation support, semantic HTML structure, screen reader announcements via live regions, and skip-to-content link. However, several requirements are partially addressed or lack complete evidence in the provided diff.

missing_requirements.reason: While dialogs have ARIA attributes, there is no evidence of focus trapping implementation (e.g., focus lock library or manual focus management code)

missing_requirements.requirement: Escape key closes dialogs and dropdowns
missing_requirements.reason: No explicit keyboard event handlers for Escape key are visible in the dialog components
While CSS variables are defined, there is no evidence that contrast ratios were actually measured or improved to meet WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
LiveRegion and useAnnouncer are implemented, but no evidence shows loading states are actually announced (e.g., 'Loading patients...')
No evidence that data table changes trigger announcements to screen readers

missing_requirements.requirement: Form validation with aria-describedby and aria-invalid across all forms
missing_requirements.reason: Only ProfileSetupModal shows proper implementation. Other forms in PatientDialog, StaffDialog, SpendingDialog are truncated or not shown with accessibility attributes
